name: Ouroboros ring: the serpent eating its own tail, the most ancient alchemical and mystical symbol. FSPLIT: the serpent bites its tail, splitting the circle into head (consumer) and tail (consumed). FFUSE: the swallowing reconstitutes the circle — head and tail are identical, the serpent feeds on itself and is fed by itself in perfect closure. VINIT: the unformed circle. TANCH: the completed ring. AFWD: the head advances. AREV: the tail retreats. ENGAGR: the serpent is simultaneously devouring and being devoured — the paradox of self-consumption. This is THE ob3ect of self-reference itself.

phase_0.domain_name: Ouroboros ring: the serpent eating its own tail, the most ancient alchemical and mystical symbol. FSPLIT: the serpent bites its tail, splitting the circle into head (consumer) and tail (consumed). FFUSE: the swallowing reconstitutes the circle — head and tail are identical, the serpent feeds on itself and is fed by itself in perfect closure. VINIT: the unformed circle. TANCH: the completed ring. AFWD: the head advances. AREV: the tail retreats. ENGAGR: the serpent is simultaneously devouring and being devoured — the paradox of self-consumption. This is THE ob3ect of self-reference itself.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the completed ring that contains the entire cycle of self-consumption

VINIT.justification: the void potential before the serpent exists or the ring is drawn

TANCH.justification: the closed boundary that contains the whole self-eating cycle

AFWD.justification: the forward morphism of the serpent's mouth moving to consume

AREV.justification: the reverse morphism of the tail being drawn into the mouth

CLINK.justification: the composition of head advancing and tail retreating into one act

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference: the serpent is both eater and eaten, identical to itself

FSPLIT.justification: the serpent bites its tail, splitting the circle into head (consumer) and tail (consumed)

FFUSE.justification: the swallowing merges head and tail back into the original ring

EVALT.justification: affirmative state: the cycle sustains itself perfectly

EVALF.justification: negative state: the ring is broken, consumption fails

ENGAGR.chosen_element: serpent simultaneously devouring and being devoured
ENGAGR.justification: paradox of self-consumption held without resolution

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of the self-eating cycle

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no serpent, no ring, pure potential
phase_3.true_description: 01 — the serpent feeds and is fed, cycle complete
phase_3.false_description: 10 — the serpent starves or the ring is broken
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the serpent is simultaneously devouring and being devoured

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the serpent recognizes itself as a whole ring
Step 2 AREV:   the tail retreats toward the mouth
Step 3 FSPLIT: the bite splits the ring into head and tail
Step 4 AFWD:   the head advances to consume the tail
Step 5 FFUSE:  the swallowing merges head and tail back into the ring
Step 6 CLINK:  the biting sequence is composed as a single act
Step 7 IFIX:   the ouroboros glyph is permanently inscribed
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the serpent recognizes itself again, now as the completed symbol

phase_5.compiler_frontend: alchemical intent — the will to self-reference
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the bite — the point of contact between head and tail
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the ring itself — the shape that remembers the cycle
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the eternal recurrence — the cycle repeats without beginning or end
phase_5.alfs_store: the ouroboros glyph as the canonical bootstrap store

phase_6.cycle_cost: nothing — the serpent feeds on itself, no external energy
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is perfectly closed, no net change
